item.title: US Treasury considers currency swap lines for Gulf and Asian allies amid Iran war fallout
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told senators that allies in the Gulf and Asia have requested dollar swap lines to stabilize markets amid economic uncertainty from the US-Iran conflict. Democrats questioned whether Trump family financial ties to UAE influenced the decision.

item.title: Prediction market Kalshi penalizes three US candidates for betting on their own races
item.description: The platform fined and suspended three unnamed political candidates for "insider trading" by wagering on their own election outcomes. The enforcement action comes amid growing calls for regulation of prediction markets.

item.title: Iran Fires on Three Ships in Strait of Hormuz as Diplomacy Stalls
item.description: Iran attacked three vessels in the strategic waterway, seizing two of them. The escalation comes as US blockade continues and diplomatic efforts to end the conflict face mounting challenges.

item.title: Rep. David Scott, Georgia Democrat, Dies at Age 80
item.description: The 13-term congressman from Georgia died while seeking re-election. Scott served on the House Financial Services Committee and was known for bipartisan efforts.

item.title: Slovakia Resumes Russian Oil Flow Through Ukraine Pipeline
item.description: Russian oil deliveries to Slovakia have resumed through the Druzhba pipeline after Ukraine unblocked the route, resolving a months-long standoff over transit fees.

item.title: Pope Leo Criticises Equatorial Guinea Prisons at End of Africa Tour
item.description: During his visit, Pope Leo delivered a frank critique of prison conditions in Equatorial Guinea, calling for reforms as he concluded his Africa tour.

item.title: India and South Korea Agree to Double Trade to Billion by 2030
item.description: The two nations signed agreements to expand cooperation in shipbuilding, defense, and AI technology, strengthening economic ties amid regional tensions.

item.title: Lebanon seeks ceasefire extension as Israel destroys homes in occupied border areas
item.description: Lebanon is asking for a one-month extension of the ceasefire with Israel during Washington talks, while Israel continues demolishing homes in southern Lebanese territory it has occupied since the truce.

item.title: Tencent releases first flagship AI model led by former OpenAI researcher
item.description: Chinese tech giant Tencent unveiled its HY3 AI model, described as its most powerful yet, developed under former OpenAI researcher Yao Shunyu. The model is open-source with 295 billion parameters.

item.title: G7 drops climate change from Paris agenda to avoid US confrontation
item.description: France confirmed climate change has been removed from the G7 environment ministers meeting agenda in Paris to prevent conflict with the United States, focusing instead on less contentious issues.

item.title: EU unblocks €90bn Ukraine loan after Hungary pipeline repaired
item.description: Ukraines Zelensky joins EU leaders in Cyprus as the bloc approves a massive loan package, ending a deadlock after Hungary lifted its veto following Kyivs repair of a Russia-damaged pipeline.

item.title: Peru ministers resign after president postpones F-16 fighter jet deal
item.description: Top Peruvian cabinet members resigned following President Boluarte decision to postpone a US-backed F-16 fighter jet purchase. The Trump administration warned the move reflects bad faith negotiations.

item.title: Taiwan Minister Visits Disputed South China Sea Island as Tensions Rise
item.description: Taiwan Ocean Affairs Minister Kuan Bi-ling made a rare visit to Itu Aba (Taiping Island) to observe coast guard drills, as US-Philippines military exercises draw Chinese anger. China dispatched a warship and sailed an aircraft carrier through the Taiwan Strait.

item.title: Japan Ends Post-WWII Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi announced Japan will begin selling lethal weapons to 17 countries, a major shift from the nation's pacifist stance amid heightened regional security concerns.

item.title: China Builds New Military Island in Disputed South China Sea Waters
item.description: Satellite imagery shows China rapidly constructing a crescent-shaped island on Antelope Reef in the Paracels. The base, nearly twice the size of New York's Central Park, will likely host airstrips, radar, and missile facilities.

item.title: Warner Bros Discovery and Paramount Merger Vote Looms Amid Regulatory Scrutiny
item.description: Shareholders are set to vote on a merger combining Warner Bros Discovery with Paramount Skydance, placing CNN and CBS News under one roof. The deal faces antitrust investigations and concerns over foreign investments from Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and UAE.

item.title: Iran Attacks Ships in Strait of Hormuz as US Reinforces Middle East
item.description: Iran has fired on commercial vessels in the strategic Strait of Hormuz after Trump extended a ceasefire, prompting the US to send thousands more troops to the region. The escalation threatens one of the worlds most critical oil shipping lanes.

item.title: US Intercepts Three Iranian Oil Tankers in Asian Waters
item.description: American forces have seized three Iranian oil tankers in the Indian Ocean, expanding the US-Iran conflict into the Indo-Pacific region. The interceptions target Irans covert oil exports to China.

item.title: Israeli Strike Kills Journalist in Lebanon Amid War Crimes Accusations
item.description: An Israeli airstrike killed a Lebanese journalist after ongoing attacks blocked rescuers from reaching the area. Lebanons prime minister accused Israel of war crimes over the targeted killing.

item.title: Navy Secretary John Phelan Fired During Iran Crisis
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has fired Navy Secretary John Phelan amid ongoing naval operations against Iran. The dismissal comes as the US maintains a naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Chemical Leak at West Virginia Plant Kills Two, Dozens Hospitalized
item.description: A chemical release at a West Virginia industrial plant killed two workers and sent at least 30 people to hospitals. Emergency crews are investigating the cause of the leak.

item.title: Germany unveils first post-WWII military strategy to become Europe's strongest army by 2039
item.description: Defense Minister Boris Pistorius presented a new national defense strategy marking Germany's first such plan since World War II, as Russia's growing threat drives European rearmament. The Bundeswehr will move from rigid hardware quotas to a flexible, effects-based planning model.

item.title: Spanish Defense Minister announces 100 armored vehicles for Ukraine during Kyiv visit
item.description: Spain will send ammunition and 100 armored vehicles to Ukraine, Defense Minister Margarita Robles announced during a visit to Kyiv, calling it a testament to Spain's commitment to democratic values and a just peace.

item.title: Navy Secretary John Phelan removed from post amid Iran war and Pentagon clashes
item.description: Trump's top Navy official John Phelan was abruptly removed from his position immediately, the Pentagon confirmed without providing a reason. The departure comes as the US Navy enforces a high-stakes blockade of Iran and follows the firing of a top US Army general.

item.title: Russia could be ready for NATO conflict within a year after Ukraine, Dutch intelligence warns
item.description: Moscow's goal would not be to defeat the alliance militarily but to divide it through limited territorial gains, according to a new Dutch intelligence report as European nations accelerate defense preparations.
